title: androsace adfinis subsp. adfinis
intro: Androsace adfinis subsp. adfinis, also known as the Tibetan rock jasmine, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Primulaceae family. It is native to the Himalayan region and is known for its delicate white or pink flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds and reduce fever. In herbal preparations, Androsace adfinis subsp. adfinis is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to provide health benefits such as reducing stress, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system.
